### Text
Abstract This memo argues that the current U.S. Special Operations Forces (SOF) targeting policy achieves tactical success but lacks strategic alignment, prioritizing rapid kinetic operations over long-term political and operational coherence. It evaluates three policy options and finds that existing frameworks fail to adequately address escalation risks, partner capacity development, and the demands of great-power competition. The memo recommends a …
